stories.title: MLBx All-Star 3-On-3 Is TOMORROW — Full Team Rosters Confirmed
stories.angle: Complete picture now visible — this isn't just softball vs. MLB legends. Four Philly icons (three 2008 World Series champions + a Hall of Fame inductee) facing a co-ed lineup of AUSL stars and Eagles stars. The story is WHO is competing against WHO.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
MLBx: All-Star 3-on-3 is TOMORROW.

AUSL stars. MLB legends. Eagles stars. Citizens Bank Park. 3 PM ET on Peacock.

This is professional softball's biggest moment of All-Star Week.

Don't miss it. #Softball #MLBx #AllStarWeek

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Full MLBx teams are locked:

Team Howard: Alyssa Brito + DeVonta Smith
Team Rollins: Tiare Jennings + Terrell Owens
Team Victorino: Rachel Garcia + Jordan Mailata
Team Jones: NiJaree Canady

Tomorrow. 3 PM ET. Peacock. #Softball #MLBx

article.headline: MLBx All-Star 3-On-3 Is Tomorrow — Full Team Rosters Confirmed

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
MLBx All-Star 3-On-3 Is TOMORROW — Full Team Rosters Confirmed

#### Image Concept 1A — Article Hero / FB Long-Form

**Concept:** Action split — two panels. Left: NiJaree Canady in her AUSL Texas Volts uniform mid-pitch or at bat. Right: Ryan Howard in his Phillies uniform at the plate or in a home run celebration. Clean dividing line between panels with "TOMORROW" overlaid in bold white text. Citizens Bank Park graphic backdrop.

**Imagn Search Terms:**
- `NiJaree Canady Texas Volts`
- `Ryan Howard Philadelphia Phillies`

**Canva Direction:** Two-photo side-by-side split, I Love Softball brand colors (purple/gold), "MLBx 3-on-3 TOMORROW" headline top, "Citizens Bank Park | July 12 | 3 PM ET | Peacock" footer bar

---

#### Image Concept 1B — Team Roster Graphic / FB Caption

**Concept:** Four-team roster breakdown graphic. Four vertical columns, each labeled with the MLB captain's name. Under each: AUSL player + NFL star, with small headshots or silhouettes if available. Bold, clean design — scoreboard-style.

**Imagn Search Terms:**
- `Alyssa Brito Carolina Blaze`
- `Tiare Jennings Texas Volts`
- `Rachel Garcia Texas Volts`
- `NiJaree Canady Texas Volts`

**Canva Direction:** Four-column grid, each column has captain name (large), AUSL player (medium), NFL star (medium), brand footer

---

research.facts:
MLBx All-Star 3-On-3 — Full Team Rosters Confirmed
**Source(s):**
- MLB.com press release: "Phillies-Braves Renewed as MLBx: All-Star 3-on-3 Presented by Ito En Reveals Final Rosters and Matchups"
- theausl.com/news/mlbx-all-star-3-on-3-canady-brito-garcia-jennings/
- Line Drive Media: "Four AUSL Stars Headed to MLB All-Star Weekend to Compete in MLBx: All-Star 3-on-3"
- mlb.com/all-star/sunday/mlbx
- Philadelphia Inquirer: "MLBx 3-on-3 game: all-star players, date, time, roster"
**Confirmed Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Event: Sunday July 12 at Citizens Bank Park, 3:00 PM ET, on Peacock
- Full team rosters confirmed via MLB.com press release:
- Team Howard: Ryan Howard (captain/MLB legend), Alyssa Brito (AUSL/Carolina Blaze), DeVonta Smith (Philadelphia Eagles WR)
- Team Rollins: Jimmy Rollins (captain/MLB legend), Tiare Jennings (AUSL/Texas Volts), Terrell Owens
- Team Victorino: Shane Victorino (captain/MLB legend), Rachel Garcia (AUSL/Texas Volts), Jordan Mailata (Philadelphia Eagles OT)
- Team Jones: Andruw Jones (captain/MLB legend, 2026 Baseball Hall of Fame inductee), NiJaree Canady (AUSL/Texas Volts)
- Format: 2 semifinals + 1 championship, ~30 minutes each; 2-minute batting window per player per game; HR = 1 point, catch = 1 point, center-field target bonus; Hot Streak power-up = 5 swings at 2x points
- Andruw Jones is 2026 Baseball Hall of Fame inductee (new context this week)
- No scratches or lineup changes indexed as of July 11
- Brito: 3x WCWS champion at Oklahoma, U.S. WNT member
- Jennings: 4x WCWS champion at Oklahoma
- Garcia: Olympic silver medalist (Tokyo 2020), 2019 WCWS champion (UCLA)
- Canady: 2026 AUSL 2nd overall pick, 2x NFCA Pitcher of the Year

stories.title: Lonni Alameda Makes Her AUSL Broadcast Debut TONIGHT — Gasso Returns Sunday
stories.angle: Alameda's debut is the story. Two of the most accomplished coaches in college softball history are calling consecutive nights of crucial AUSL games during the sport's biggest broadcast week ever. This is the AUSL's identity moment.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Lonni Alameda makes her AUSL broadcast debut TONIGHT.

8:00 PM ET on MLB.TV — Talons vs. Spark.

Gasso debuted Thursday. Alameda tonight. Gasso again Sunday.

Two legends. Three nights. The best week in softball's broadcast history. #AUSL #Softball

article.headline: Lonni Alameda Makes Her AUSL Broadcast Debut Tonight — Gasso Returns Sunday

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Lonni Alameda Makes Her AUSL Broadcast Debut TONIGHT

#### Image Concept 2A — Article Hero / FB Long-Form

**Concept:** Broadcast milestone graphic. Photo of Lonni Alameda (Florida State sideline or press conference context). Overlay text: "BROADCAST DEBUT TONIGHT" with AUSL branding. Subtle headset/microphone icon integrated into design. 8 PM ET / MLB.TV info at bottom.

**Imagn Search Terms:**
- `Lonni Alameda Florida State`

**Canva Direction:** Dark background, I Love Softball purple, spotlight effect on Alameda photo, "Alameda | AUSL Broadcast Debut" headline, "Tonight | 8 PM ET | MLB.TV" subhead

---

#### Image Concept 2B — Gasso + Alameda Duo Graphic

**Concept:** Two-panel graphic — Patty Gasso (left) and Lonni Alameda (right). "Two Legends. Three Nights." headline. Show debut dates: Gasso July 9, Alameda July 11, Gasso July 12. Reinforces the broadcast milestone arc.

**Imagn Search Terms:**
- `Patty Gasso Oklahoma`
- `Lonni Alameda Florida State`

**Canva Direction:** Two headshots side by side, dates listed below each, "The Best Booth Week in Softball History" subtext, AUSL logo watermark

---

research.facts:
Lonni Alameda AUSL Broadcast Debut Tonight
**Source(s):**
- OurSports Central: "Patty Gasso, Lonni Alameda to Join AUSL Booth this Week"
- theausl.com/news/ausl-announces-2026-broadcast-schedule/
- AUSL official schedule page (theausl.com/schedule/)
**Confirmed Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Lonni Alameda (FSU head coach) makes AUSL broadcast debut TONIGHT, Saturday July 11, at 8:00 PM ET on MLB.TV
- Game: Utah Talons vs. Oklahoma City Spark at Tom Heath Field, Oklahoma City, OK
- Patty Gasso (Oklahoma HC, 8 national titles): debuted July 9 on ESPNU; returns July 12 on MLB Network
- Today's full AUSL schedule (Saturday July 11):
- 2:00 PM ET — Carolina Blaze vs. Texas Volts (Dell Diamond, Round Rock TX)
- 5:00 PM ET — Chicago Bandits vs. Portland Cascade (Hillsboro Ballpark, Hillsboro OR)
- 8:00 PM ET — Utah Talons vs. Oklahoma City Spark (Tom Heath Field, Oklahoma City OK) — Alameda in booth on MLB.TV
- No cancellation or schedule change reported for Alameda's July 11 appearance

stories.title: AUSL Final 9 — Three Games Today, No. 1 Seed Race Enters Critical Week
stories.angle: Three AUSL games in one day means the standings will look different tonight than they do this morning. Who gains? Who falls back? The final 9-game sprint starts today.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Three AUSL games today:

🥎 2:00 PM ET — Blaze vs. Volts (Dell Diamond, TX)
🥎 5:00 PM ET — Bandits vs. Cascade (Hillsboro Ballpark, OR)
🥎 8:00 PM ET — Talons vs. Spark (Tom Heath Field, OKC)

9 games left. No. 1 seed earns a bye to the Championship. #AUSL #Softball

article.headline: AUSL Final 9 — Three Games Today, No. 1 Seed Race Enters Critical Week

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
AUSL Final 9 — Three Games Today

#### Image Concept 3A — Schedule Graphic / X Post + FB

**Concept:** Today's three-game schedule presented as a countdown graphic. Three time slots stacked vertically: 2 PM, 5 PM, 8 PM with matchup names and venues. "3 GAMES. 1 DAY." headline. Clean, game-day feel.

**Imagn Search Terms:**
- `Utah Talons softball`
- `Chicago Bandits softball`

**Canva Direction:** Dark background, clock/ball icon, three matchup rows with time + teams + venue, "9 Games Left | No. 1 Seed Earns Bye" footer

---

#### Image Concept 3B — Standings Race Graphic

**Concept:** AUSL standings scoreboard-style graphic. Utah Talons at top with "LEADS" badge. "9 Games Left" countdown. No. 1 seed = bye to Championship Series callout. Creates urgency and clarity for casual fans.

**Imagn Search Terms:**
- `AUSL softball stadium`
- `softball championship`

**Canva Direction:** Scoreboard aesthetic, team names in rank order, countdown number prominent, playoff path callout at bottom

---

research.facts:
AUSL Standings Race — 9 Games Left
**Source(s):**
- theausl.com/standings/
- OurSports Central: AUSL schedule pages
**Confirmed Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Utah Talons in first place (confirmed; multiple sources)
- Regular season ends July 20 (9 games remaining from today)
- No. 1 seed earns direct bye to Championship Series (July 25-27, Davis Diamond, College Station TX), skipping July 23 play-in
- Today's three-game AUSL slate: Blaze vs. Volts (2 PM ET), Bandits vs. Cascade (5 PM ET), Talons vs. Spark (8 PM ET)
**Confirmed Facts (MEDIUM confidence):**
- Talons were 14-5 through July 9; July 10 game results not confirmed in indexed sources
- Chicago Bandits in second place, chasing Talons; were 10-7 heading into July 10 games

stories.title: Megan Grant ESPYS — 4 Days to Vote
stories.angle: Four votes remain. Each day is irreplaceable. Wednesday is the night.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
4 days left to vote for Megan Grant at the ESPYs.

42 home runs. A 31-year record. She's up for Best Record-Breaking Performance.

One vote per day at ESPN.com through July 15.

Wednesday night. ABC. Use every vote you have left. #Softball #ESPYs

article.headline: Megan Grant ESPYS — 4 Days Left to Vote for Best Record-Breaking Performance

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Megan Grant ESPYS — 4 Days to Vote

#### Image Concept 4A — Vote CTA Graphic / FB + X

**Concept:** Megan Grant action photo (batting or pitching at UCLA or in Portland Cascade uniform) with bold "VOTE NOW" CTA. "4 Days Left" countdown. ESPN.com vote link prominently displayed. ESPYs logo integration.

**Imagn Search Terms:**
- `Megan Grant UCLA`
- `Megan Grant Portland Cascade`

**Canva Direction:** Gold/purple I Love Softball palette, "VOTE FOR MEGAN GRANT" headline, "Best Record-Breaking Performance | 34th ESPYs" subhead, "ESPN.com | Once Per Day | Through July 15" footer

---

#### Image Concept 4B — Record Graphic

**Concept:** Infographic showing the home run record progression. Laura Espinoza: 37 HR (1995, Arizona). Megan Grant: 42 HR (2026, UCLA). Simple bar comparison with "31-Year Record BROKEN" headline. Makes the achievement visceral.

**Imagn Search Terms:**
- `Megan Grant UCLA batting`

**Canva Direction:** Clean stats graphic, two-bar comparison, years and player names clearly labeled, "Nominated: Best Record-Breaking Performance | 34th ESPYs" bottom banner

---

research.facts:
Megan Grant ESPYS — 4 Days to Vote
**Source(s):**
- theausl.com/cascade/news/vote-megan-grant-2026-espy/
- uclabruins.com/news/2026/6/26/womens-basketball-vote-now-betts-grant-nominated-for-the-2026-espys
- SI.com: "NCAA Softball Highlighted in 2026 ESPYs Awards Nominations"
- ESPN Press Room: 2026 ESPYS nominees list
**Confirmed Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Category: Best Record-Breaking Performance
- Grant's feat: 42 HR in 2026 at UCLA, breaking Laura Espinoza's 1995 Arizona record of 37 HR
- Grant is now playing for Portland Cascade (No. 4 overall pick, 2026 AUSL College Draft)
- Also set UCLA career HR record: 91 HR, passing Stacey Nuveman
- Competitors: Myles Garrett (NFL/LA Rams), Johannes Høsflot Klæbo (cross-country skiing), Sabastian Sawe (Kenya, long-distance running)
- Voting: Once per day at ESPN.com through July 15 = 4 days remaining (July 12, 13, 14, 15)
- 34th annual ESPYs air Wednesday July 15, 8 PM ET on ABC and ESPN app
- Host: Marcello Hernández

stories.title: PGF Nationals 8 Days — Roster Freeze in 2 Days (July 13)
stories.angle: Two days to the roster freeze, not three — the window is closing faster than many families realize. Late roster moves and pickups must happen TODAY or tomorrow.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
2 days until the PGF Nationals roster freeze.

July 13 is the deadline — Monday at 11:59 PM PST.

Today and tomorrow are the last windows to make roster moves.

July 19 is first pitch at Great Park, Irvine. Don't miss the freeze. #PGFNationals #Softball

article.headline: PGF Nationals Roster Freeze Is in 2 Days — What Coaches and Families Need to Know

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
PGF Nationals — 8 Days, Roster Freeze in 2 Days

#### Image Concept 5A — Freeze Deadline Graphic / FB + X

**Concept:** Countdown graphic with urgency. "ROSTER FREEZE: 2 DAYS" in large bold type. July 13, 11:59 PM PST deadline clearly displayed. PGF Nationals logo or Great Park Irvine visual. Call to action: "Make your moves TODAY."

**Imagn Search Terms:**

- `youth softball tournament`

**Canva Direction:** Red/orange urgency palette, clock icon, freeze date large and prominent, "PGF Nationals | July 19 | Great Park, Irvine" event callout below

research.facts:
PGF Nationals — 8 Days, Roster Freeze 2 Days
**Source(s):**
- premiergirlsfastpitch.com/home.php (PGF Rules and Regulations)
- pgfnationals.com/week-one
**Confirmed Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Event: July 19, Great Park, Irvine (8000 Great Park Blvd, Irvine CA 92618)
- Week One: 18U and 16U
- Roster freeze: July 13 at 11:59 PM PST (2 days from today, not tomorrow — July 12 is the day before the freeze)
- Transfer portal open through July 31
- Automatic releases active since July 1 (non-qualified/non-invited team players auto-released)
- September 1 contact window for Class of 2028 opens in 52 days
